title: Prosperity Club — Financial Education
description: Deborah Armstrong's Prosperity Club. Practical financial education, insurance guidance, and the discipline of managing money you actually have.

sections.title: How to make enough money to pay your bills.
sections.lede: Practical financial education led by Deborah Armstrong — licensed insurance agent. The discipline of managing money you actually have.

background.alt: Wide editorial photograph evoking quiet financial deliberation

sections.text: Prosperity Club exists to teach people the discipline of managing the money they have — not chasing the money they don't. Read the columns. Watch the master classes. Ask the questions you've never asked an insurance agent in person.

sections.caption: Personal finance is not a spreadsheet exercise; it is a daily discipline.

items.value: How class shapes the financial choices you don't see.
items.hint: Where money habits actually come from.

items.value: Renters / homeowners / valuables coverage without the upsell.
items.hint: What insurance actually pays — and what it doesn't.

items.value: Index funds, IRA basics, the math that beats the brokerage.
items.hint: Start with cash flow, not stock picks.

sections.body: If you only learn one thing here: cash flow is the foundation. Everything else is decoration on a house you don't own yet.

sections.caption: Discretionary income is where most household budgets quietly leak.

sections.text: Financial literacy in the US is fragmented across hundreds of paywalled platforms, none of which talk to each other. Most ask you to subscribe before they tell you what you'd learn. Prosperity Club is the opposite: every column is free, every master-class video is free, and every question gets a direct answer.

What you'll find here grows out of one career working with people who needed insurance they could afford and money decisions that didn't compound the wrong way. The club is what that experience looks like when it's written down.

items.value: Curated columns on personal finance, insurance, and the math behind both.
Free is free. No subscriptions, no upsells, no "premium tier."
items.hint: If you want personal guidance, contact directly.

items.value: Master-class videos cover the same material as a paid seminar.
Where the money you control after fixed expenses actually goes.
items.hint: Most of it leaks before you notice.

sections.text: How to make better money decisions
Start with what you have, not what you wish you had. Three habits, in order:

items.definition: Track every dollar that moves through your account for one month. The picture you draw from that month is the only one that matters.
Renters / health / disability / term life. The downside protection has to exist before the upside math is allowed to compound.
Every policy. Every contract. Every "first year free" trial. The fee schedule is where the answer is.

sections.body: Learn to control cash on hand. Everything else follows.
